text.title: hooks Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for hooks as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Exit code 2 blocks the tool — if your hook crashes unexpectedly (Python exception), it exits non-zero and blocks silently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always wrap JSON parsing in try/except. Malformed input should default to allowing (exit 0), not blocking. Test hooks manually before deploying.

failure-mode.description: Hooks run synchronously; a slow hook (API call, heavy computation) adds latency to every tool call it matches.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Set aggressive timeouts: "timeout": 10 for simple checks, never leave default 60s. Keep hook logic under 30 lines. Delegate complex logic to agents or background scripts.

failure-mode.description: suppressOutput: true in the hook JSON response hides the permission prompt — accidentally suppressing all output makes debugging hard.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use suppressOutput sparingly. When suppressing, always log the decision to a file (~/.claude/hook-audit.log) for visibility.

failure-mode.description: Regex matchers in matcher field must be valid JavaScript regex; PCRE syntax doesn't work.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Test matcher patterns in JavaScript environment first. Prefer simple exact matches ("Bash") over complex regex when possible.

failure-mode.description: Hooks defined in component frontmatter (skills/agents) run only while that component is active — not globally.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: For global hooks, register in ~/.claude/settings.json or .claude/settings.json. Document component-scoped hooks clearly in the skill/agent README.

failure-mode.description: The CLAUDE_ENV_FILE trick (persist env vars) only works in SessionStart hooks; other hooks cannot persist env between calls.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: For persistent state, write to a file or use a shared JSON config. SessionStart is the only hook that can persist environment variables across sessions.

failure-mode.description: An agent that writes hook scripts and registers them cannot use them in the same session — hooks load at startup only.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Document that agents must restart the session or continue with a new session to use newly-installed hooks. Plan multi-session workflows when hook installation is part of the task.

failure-mode.description: If a PreToolUse hook blocks a tool the agent considers essential, the agent may enter a retry loop.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Design hooks to provide actionable error messages explaining what alternative to use. Never block without guidance.

failure-mode.description: Hooks with permissionDecision: "allow" bypass the normal permission prompt — this is powerful but means the user has no visibility into what was auto-approved.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Log these approvals to ~/.claude/hook-audit.log so the user can audit what was auto-approved and revoke it if needed.

failure-mode.description: The updatedInput field in PreToolUse output lets a hook rewrite the command Claude executes — powerful but can cause unexpected behavior if the agent doesn't know the command was modified.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use updatedInput sparingly. Always log or display what modification was made. Never silently modify input in ways that change semantics.

failure-mode.description: Stop hooks that return {"decision": "block"} prevent the agent from completing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always include a "reason" so the agent knows why it was blocked and what to do next.

failure-mode.description: UserPromptSubmit hooks run before Claude sees the prompt — a hook that blocks here silently drops the user's message.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always show a reason in stopReason so the user knows what happened.
